title: Pillar Song
desc: The inspection gantry circles the Iron Pillar on hanging catwalks, and tonight the lamp cages and warning plates need to be set before the work shift climbs.

steps.text:
The Iron Pillar does not merely stand in the UnderWorld. It occupies it. The metal column rises out of haze and furnace glow like something too large to have been built by anyone currently alive, its surface banded with old patchwork, fresh braces, and the dark seams where heat and stress have argued for centuries. Inspection catwalks ring it at intervals, hung out over the drop on chains that hum faintly when the pillar's magnetic field shifts. Far below, if you lean over the inner rail at the wrong moment, you can see the huge outline of the Gigantroper still bearing its impossible burden.

The gantry keeper checks the lamp chest with one hand braced against the railing. "Clean and light the cage lamps if you want the safer job," she says. "Or realign the warning plates along the inner rail if you prefer to work where the hum is strongest. Either way, the shift needs to read the pillar before it climbs onto it."

options.label: Clean and light the cage lamps around the gantry
Realign the warning plates along the inner rail

steps.text:
The cage lamps have gathered black dust and rust bloom from the last tremor, enough to turn guidance into suggestion. You unhook each one, wipe the glass, trim the wick, and relight the flame so it burns steady instead of guttering under its own soot. One by one, warm circles of light appear along the catwalk, catching chain links, rail teeth, and the pillar's great curved surface in brief readable sections.

When the whole ring is lit, the gantry stops feeling like a chain hung in darkness and starts feeling like part of a system built to make the impossible traversable. The keeper watches the lamp line chase itself around the pillar and exhales. "Better. If something this large is going to terrify people, it should at least do so with good lighting."

options.label: Latch the lamp chest and leave the gantry burning clear

steps.text:
The warning plates are narrow iron tongues mounted along the inner rail where crews can read field strength by the angle they pull and the little vibrating note they make when the hum deepens. Two have twisted off true and another has started chattering against its bracket instead of singing cleanly. You loosen the mounts, tip each plate back into alignment, and test the line until the tones fall in proper progression from one section to the next.

Once corrected, the rail begins telling the truth again. Near the stronger bands the plates lean and shiver. Near the calmer spans they settle to a lower hum. The keeper listens down the line with her head tilted, reading the sound as much as the metal. "Good," she says. "A pillar this dangerous should never be allowed to keep secrets out of laziness."

options.label: Set the wrench aside and leave the rail speaking properly
